what_it_does: Declares the 'repo' content lens/projection as a module-level PROJECTION dictionary: it marks repo-file digests as embeddable text (embeds:true, field:text, stage:legibility) so they can be placed in a vector space named 'repo' for retrieval.
observations: The file contains only a docstring and a PROJECTION dictionary; no executable functions or classes.
PROJECTION has id='repo', level='content', produced_by='model', embeds=True, field='text', and stage='legibility'.
The docstring frames this as the 'repo-exocortex content lens' that 'DOES embed' and becomes a vector space.
It references an external 'repo_digest' role that produces per-file digests landing in this space.
It documents a specific unblock rationale ('G15 unblock') for capture(projection='repo').
It states the projection id MUST equal the file stem ('repo').
It repeats a 'render-not-judge' directive for the description content.
standouts: embeds:true makes this a declared vector space for repo-level retrieval.
The id must equal the file stem ('repo').
It explicitly ties its existence to unblocking capture(projection='repo').
The render-not-judge principle is embedded in the description.
conventions: Projection declared as a module-level PROJECTION dict.
File stem matches the projection id.
Docstring carries design rationale, cross-references, and an 'unblock' narrative rather than only API docs.
Uses Cognition Engine K1 / exocortex / Group L terminology consistently.
concerns: Heavy jargon and external references (G15, Group L, repo_digest, runtime/projections.py) make the file hard to understand in isolation.
No in-file validation that id equals file stem or that embeds:true is honored.
The actual embedding mechanism and digest format are not defined here.
The docstring is longer than the code it describes.
notes: This file registers the embeddable 'repo' projection so per-file digests can be stored in a vector space for retrieval.
questions: How is the embedding for this space actually computed and stored?
What is the exact contract of the repo_digest role referenced in the docstring?
How does runtime/projections.py consume this PROJECTION dict?
What does stage='legibility' imply for downstream processing?
How is the 'space=repo' name enforced across the system?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual: it declares the repo lens/projection configuration and the embeddable space for repo digests.
intention_signals: It is trying to formally establish a retrieval-ready repo embedding space and remove the failure path for capture(projection='repo') by declaring the lens explicitly.
novelty: It explicitly frames a projection as a vector space via embeds:true and documents the declaration as a system-unblock event (G15).
contribution: It contributes the declaration that repo file digests are embeddable and retrievable under the 'repo' space, enabling find_relations/retrieve to range over repo content.
summary_for_embedding: projections/repo.py declares the embeddable 'repo' content lens (PROJECTION id='repo', embeds=true, field='text', stage='legibility') so per-file repo digests can be embedded into a vector space for retrieval.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to formally register the repo projection and unblock capture(projection='repo') by making repo file summaries a retrievable vector space.
